summary: Prodigy Digital Library — a digital library platform on the Internet Computer with four roles (Guest, Reader, Author, Admin). Admin principal ID: zhpm4-xby3w-hy7nv-ziwxz-heeqw-f2qf4-p2o5a-qradb. Builds have repeatedly failed due to complexity; currently attempting to build the Admin system in focused steps.
architectural_notes: Platform runs on Internet Computer (ICP); Admin principal ID is the canister controller identity
Authentication via Internet Identity; reading is open to guests, login required for uploads and interactions
Role-based access control: Guest < Reader < Author < Admin; strict backend enforcement
known_issues: Full platform build repeatedly fails due to complexity; building Admin system in focused incremental steps

existing_features.title: Build a complete Admin Dashboard page at the `/admin` route, protected by the existing `AdminGuard` component. The dashboard must contain clearly separated sections reachable via a sidebar or tab navigation: Overview/Analytics, User Management, Book Management, Storage Control, Platform Settings, and Security. The UI must be clean, modern, and fully mobile-responsive.

Implement the Analytics/Overview section of the Admin Dashboard. Display stat cards for: total registered users, total books, total uploads, total approved books, total pending books. Display a list of the most-viewed books and most-bookmarked books. Display most-active users by upload count.

Implement the User Management section of the Admin Dashboard. Show a paginated or scrollable table of all registered users with columns: display name, principal ID, role, status (active/banned/suspended), books uploaded, join date. Provide action buttons per row to: delete user, ban/unban user, suspend/unsuspend user. Confirm destructive actions with a confirmation dialog before calling the backend.

Implement the Book Management section of the Admin Dashboard. Show a searchable, filterable list of all books (including pending and approved) with cover thumbnail, title, author, genre, status (approved/pending/rejected), featured flag, and view count. Provide per-book actions: edit (title, description, genre, cover URL), delete, approve, reject, feature/unfeature. The admin upload form should reuse or mirror the existing Upload page form.

Implement the Security section of the Admin Dashboard. Display a list of currently banned and suspended users with their status and the ability to unban/unsuspend from this view. Add a searchable account-blocking form where the admin can enter a principal ID to block (ban) that account. Display a platform activity log showing recent events: new user registrations, book uploads, and books removed — with timestamps and actor principal.

new_feature_requests.summary: Guest role: browse, search, read books without login
Reader role: bookmarks, reading progress, reviews, comments (requires Internet Identity login)
Author role: upload PDFs, edit up to 3x, delete own books, author analytics (requires login)
Admin system: Internet Identity auth gated to principal zhpm4-xby3w-hy7nv-ziwxz-heeqw-f2qf4-p2o5a-qradb with full platform control
Admin Dashboard: user management (view, delete, ban, suspend, activity)
Admin Dashboard: book management (upload, edit, delete any book, approve/reject, feature on homepage)
Admin Dashboard: PDF & storage control (view/delete/replace PDFs, monitor storage, enforce upload limits)
Admin Dashboard: platform settings (name, logo, homepage content, announcements, maintenance mode)
Admin Dashboard: security control (monitor activity, force logout, block accounts) and analytics panel (users, books, uploads, popular content, growth)
Book publishing: immediate visibility after upload; Admin moderates by removal

Extend the backend Motoko actor to support admin-only operations gated to principal ID `zhpm4-xby3w-hy7nv-ziwxz-heeqw-f2qf4-p2o5a-qradb`: get all users with activity info, delete a user, ban/unban a user, suspend/unsuspend a user; delete any book regardless of owner; approve or reject a book (set approval status); feature/unfeature a book on the homepage; get all uploaded PDFs with metadata; delete or replace a PDF; get storage usage stats and enforce a configurable max upload size; get and set platform settings (site name, logo URL, announcement banner text, maintenance mode toggle); force-logout a user (invalidate session marker); get analytics data (total users, total books, total uploads, most-viewed books, most-bookmarked books, new users and books over time). All admin endpoints must verify the caller's principal matches the hardcoded admin principal and trap/return an error otherwise.

Implement the Storage Control section of the Admin Dashboard. Display aggregate storage usage (total bytes used across all PDFs) and the configured maximum upload size. Show a list of all uploaded PDF entries with file name, associated book title, uploader, file size, and upload date. Provide a delete button per PDF entry and a global control to update the maximum allowed upload size.

Implement the Platform Settings section of the Admin Dashboard. Provide an editable form with fields for: site name, logo URL, homepage announcement banner text (with an enable/disable toggle), and a maintenance mode toggle switch. Saving the form calls the backend setPlatformSettings endpoint. When maintenance mode is enabled, non-admin users visiting the frontend should see a maintenance page instead of the normal app.
